All of that. All of that goodness. Alright. Okay so I'm assuming you saw the Facebook post. Alright. Excellent. So let me kind of expand on that a little bit. And then from there we'll discuss further. So first things first. So kind of why we went in this direction. So we decided to go in this direction because we found that a majority of the clients we were working with we were not getting them the long term results that we wanted to see for them. And when you when everyone initially comes in and signs up with us can I bother you? Okay. I'm like messing with my hair so. You're like bothering me. I'm kind of bothered. So I'm just gonna say maybe you need some... anyway you know what I got here. So anyway the reason we're going that direction was because people weren't getting the results and when people came to us initially to sign up they came here with the intention of getting the results. Like they had a visualization in mind and we wanted to make that happen. But what starts to happen is we found that Ninja Fitness Academy became this block that people were trying to fit into their lives. Right? Do this template, do these workouts, do this, do that. And so as people tried to fit that template into their life they started to kind of run into these issues that needed a little bit more customization. But due to the structures we had in place we weren't necessarily able to provide that ongoing support as well as making the appropriate check-ins and program modifications for each individual person. So that's when we started our program working with people and we said okay instead of letting them depend because what happens was people were depending upon coming here and that workout being the workout that would ding all those mistakes they made while they weren't here. They're like I'm going to go to the NFA four times a week. I'm going to work out so hard that it doesn't matter what I did when I wasn't there that I'm going to make up everything. Are you reading my diary? Yes. And so we just found ourselves kind of hitting this wall as a business model. Right? We weren't able to keep people because we suddenly seeing the only value being when they were showing up here. And that's not what we wanted to do. We can't win the gym game and we don't want to play the gym game. That's not what we do for people. What we do is we provide a much more personal touch. So with that with being able to provide a more personal touch to life coaching clients we're able to modify workout programs, we're able to modify nutrition programs, we're able to modify their lifestyle and make the changes necessary so that fitness instead of being this block kind of becomes this liquid that flows into their life. And it creates a sustainable model. And the big scary thing is we don't want people to be like, if I don't go to the NFA I'm not going to be fit. And it's like that's what we don't want. We don't want people being so relied, relied, relying upon us being there for them that they are unable to do it on their own. So that's basically why we went this direction. Let's teach people how to fish so that we can be there for them, provide the support, provide everything they need, eventually get into the point where it's kind of like you're your parent so it best fits that as a parent to kind of step back, step back, step back and hold that. They absorb those principles and then any time they hit the wall or they mess up that's when you come back and you go, okay cool, how do we mess up, what cost us mess up, where do you think we went away from what we wanted to do? So that's kind of the approach and the philosophy behind this type of coaching versus let's work with you four times, five times when you do coming in and let's make that the central focus. It's not that that's bad, it's just that fitness is one piece of a massive possible. Yeah. And we did the fitness part really, really right, but we did all the other parts really, really wrong. And so now we want to get all those pieces right. That would take really, really wrong. Thank you, I appreciate that. But really what I want you to do is I want you to see nothing. I want to see no spectrums. I want to see just food. And I want you to see the contributions of what those foods are doing in your body. And that's going to better educate you on when it's okay to have pizza and when pizza is not okay. When it's okay to have ice cream, when ice cream is not okay. And assuming your body is not like lactose intolerance. But calories are calories. And so that's what the program allows us to do is provide that in in-depth touch and modification is necessary. And then generally your coaches will touch basically about four times a week. One time in the week. It'll be nutrition, workouts, happiness, motivation. It's going to be in-depth check-ins. It won't just be like, hey, hope it's going great. See you later. It's going to be like, hey, how's it going? How's your workouts going? How's your nutrition going? And then to be honest, the best way to get the most out of it is just be honest with the coach. Really, I'm struggling. Like, I'm struggling. Like, I'm finding that I'm binge-eating or I'm stressing at night or whatever. And like, whatever those things, those demons that are haunting you, there is a way to modify your plan so that you can better battle those demons and not feel like such a large task. It just requires the right tool. Which is just a shift in behavior, a shift in pattern. Or changing to an intermittent fasting style. Whatever it is, there are modifications that can be made. There are a couple things that are going to happen. People are going to show for open gym and the life coaches will be here. So those people are going to kind of advance on the life coaches and then it's going to interrupt the life coaches schedule because they're going to have a lot of people to be maintaining. So their day-to-day is going to be just like, okay, I need to call Caroline. I haven't talked to her in two days. She's not responding. I got to call her. So like, let's say we have ten people doing open gym over there and then we're like, okay, we're going to run a one-on-four and then now like, people are kind of like, okay, can I jump in? Now it's a one-on-ten and one-on-fifth and it's like, gosh, this is defeating the point. So in a way, we thought about it but it was just a very self-defeating thing to try to do logistically from where we're coming as a business. We understand that that is somewhat an inconvenience for everyone but we are hoping to provide ample amounts of value in other categories so that you guys are just psyched out for that. So. I'm just thinking, overall people, if they came here to work out and now we can no longer work out then they're going to be like, well, now I got to pay for another gym to go work out and then they might kind of see that's the feeling of this too. Sure, and you know the reality is a lot of our clients are paying a reduced fee to the point where they could afford a ten-dollar membership at like Planet Fitness or like Chuzz or Choose or however you say it, I don't see it as Chuzz but yeah, so like more and more of those gyms are kind of popping up and they're squeezing the small business gyms like small fitness businesses like ours out of the picture because there's no way we can keep your people in a nice ass facility like that. It's ten bucks a month. Just no hooking, like they have classes and all this stuff.
